mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that remains below its trailing 12-month average signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.61% is below prior tightening peaks
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS deviation from its 12-month average and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 3-month low, a Sahm-rule-like recession signal is activated, causing investors to reprice equity risk downward and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where the 0.3pp UNRATE trigger fires, invalidating the recession-signal mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and RSAFS both accelerate simultaneously (positive month-over-month change in each), the joint confirmation of production and consumption growth signals a synchronized expansion phase, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days that exceed what either series predicts alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and RSAFS month-over-month changes adds no incremental Rank IC beyond either single-series model over any 2-year rolling window (delta IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months), a simultaneous decline in HOUST reflects rate-sensitive housing demand contraction that leads broader demand deterioration and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; the same HOUST decline in an easing cycle carries no such signal.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following triggered HOUST declines during tightening regimes are not significantly more negative than HOUST declines during easing regimes (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market strength that supports near-term consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3%, elevated inflation raises the probability of future monetary tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the CPI trigger is active, contradicting the tightening-repricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising FEDFUNDS and rising UNRATE simultaneously signals a policy-error regime where tightening occurs despite labor market deterioration, amplifying recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter beyond what either series indicates independently.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-UNRATE interaction signal fails to produce incremental Rank IC beyond 0.02 relative to the single-series UNRATE trigger over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trailing 3-month trend in RSAFS reflects sustained consumer spending momentum that supports near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month trend and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across any rolling 2-year window.
